text:
There once was a girl named Alice who always got lost. Her best friend was a kind, tiny red mouse with brown stripes named Benny. Benny would run alongside Alice and chatter away, telling her stories.

One moonlit night, Alice decided to go walking by herself. She wandered into a mysterious forest. As she moved deeper into the woods, she felt a chill but pressed on. Benny was nowhere to be seen.

After a while, a strange glow caught her eye. She stepped forward, the light pulling her deeper. It was a cave, ancient and dark. There was a single flickering light at the end.

Entering, she noticed tall candles and something that looked like a big, glowing bed. On it lay a woman with skin like polished ivory. She smiled at Alice.

"Welcome," she whispered. "You seek peace?"

"I-I think so," Alice confessed.

"I've listened to your stories," the woman continued. "You miss home."

Alice didn't know how to respond
